  "text": "## Envoy v2 API Deprecated References\n\nThis analysis identified all files in the envoyproxy/envoy and envoyproxy/data-plane-api repositories that reference the deprecated Envoy v2 API namespace `envoy.api.v2`. The v2 xDS API was deprecated in Envoy 1.19 and replaced by the v3 API.\n\n### Summary of Findings\n\n#### Core v2 API Definition Files\nThe main body of deprecated v2 API definitions are located in the `api/envoy/api/v2/` directory hierarchy in the envoy repository (or `envoy/api/v2/` in the data-plane-api repository). These proto files contain the primary definitions of the v2 xDS API including:\n\n**Top-level xDS service definitions (39 files):**\n- `rds.proto`, `cds.proto`, `eds.proto`, `lds.proto`, `srds.proto`: Main xDS discovery service definitions\n- `route.proto`, `cluster.proto`, `listener.proto`, `endpoint.proto`, `scoped_route.proto`, `discovery.proto`: Core resource type definitions\n\n**Core package (`api/envoy/api/v2/core/`):**\nContains fundamental building blocks used by xDS resources, including:\n- `base.proto`, `address.proto`, `config_source.proto`: Basic configuration structures\n- `grpc_service.proto`, `health_check.proto`, `http_uri.proto`: Service-related configurations  \n- `backoff.proto`, `event_service_config.proto`, `socket_option.proto`: Advanced configuration options\n- `grpc_method_list.proto`, `protocol.proto`: Method and protocol definitions\n\n**Auth package (`api/envoy/api/v2/auth/`):**\n- `tls.proto`, `cert.proto`, `common.proto`, `secret.proto`: TLS and authentication configurations\n\n**Listener package (`api/envoy/api/v2/listener/`):**\n- `listener.proto`, `listener_components.proto`: Listener configuration components\n- `udp_listener_config.proto`, `quic_config.proto`: UDP and QUIC-specific configurations\n\n**Endpoint package (`api/envoy/api/v2/endpoint/`):**\n- `endpoint.proto`, `endpoint_components.proto`, `load_report.proto`: Endpoint and load-reporting configurations\n\n**Route package (`api/envoy/api/v2/route/`):**\n- `route.proto`, `route_components.proto`: Route configuration components\n\n**Cluster package (`api/envoy/api/v2/cluster/`):**\n- `circuit_breaker.proto`, `filter.proto`, `outlier_detection.proto`: Cluster-specific configurations\n\n**RateLimit package (`api/envoy/api/v2/ratelimit/`):**\n- `ratelimit.proto`: Rate limiting configuration\n\n#### Config Files Importing v2 API (26 files)\n\nThese files are v2-versioned config extensions that directly import from the v2 API namespace:\n\n**Trace and metrics services:**\n- `config/trace/v2/service.proto`, `config/trace/v2alpha/xray.proto`\n- `config/metrics/v2/metrics_service.proto`\n- `service/trace/v2/trace_service.proto`, `service/metrics/v2/metrics_service.proto`\n\n**Network filters:**\n- `config/filter/network/direct_response/v2/config.proto`\n- `config/filter/network/client_ssl_auth/v2/client_ssl_auth.proto`\n- `config/filter/network/local_rate_limit/v2alpha/local_rate_limit.proto`\n- `config/filter/network/ext_authz/v2/ext_authz.proto` (data-plane-api only)\n\n**Other configuration modules:**\n- `config/ratelimit/v2/rls.proto`: Rate limit service definition\n- `admin/v2alpha/listeners.proto`: Admin API for listeners\n- `config/retry/omit_host_metadata/v2/omit_host_metadata_config.proto`: Retry policy configuration\n- `config/grpc_credential/v2alpha/file_based_metadata.proto`: gRPC credential handling\n- `config/transport_socket/tap/v2alpha/tap.proto`: Tap transport socket (packet capture)\n\n**Data definitions:**\n- `data/tap/v2alpha/http.proto`: HTTP-specific tap data definitions\n- `service/tap/v2alpha/tap.proto`: Tap service definition\n\n#### C++ Source Files (7 files in envoy repository)\n\nThese C++ files contain references to the v2 API namespace in code:\n\n1. **Header files:**\n   - `test/config/v2_link_hacks.h`: Link hacks to ensure v2 API symbols are included\n   - `source/common/config/api_version.h`: API version handling utilities\n   - `envoy/config/grpc_mux.h`: gRPC API mux with v2 API documentation\n   - `envoy/upstream/host_description.h`: Host description with v2 load balancing weight references\n   - `envoy/upstream/upstream.h`: Upstream configuration with v2 endpoint references\n\n2. **Implementation files:**\n   - `api/test/build/build_test.cc`: Verification of v2 service and method names\n   - `test/test_common/utility_test.cc`: Tests using v2 type URLs\n   - `source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc`: Type URL documentation referencing v2\n   - `source/common/listener_manager/listener_manager_impl.cc`: Listener implementation with v2 API references\n\n#### Configuration Examples (1 file)\n- `configs/using_deprecated_config.yaml`: YAML configuration example using deprecated v2 API features\n\n### Repository Distribution\n\n**envoyproxy/envoy:** 61 files total\n- Proto definitions: 50 files\n- C++ source: 7 files  \n- Configuration examples: 1 file\n- Documentation: 3 files\n\n**envoyproxy/data-plane-api:** 48 files total\n- Proto definitions: 48 files (mirrors the proto structure from envoy but without C++ files)\n\n### Key Observations\n\n1. The v2 API is pervasive throughout the codebase, with the entire xDS discovery service and configuration system built around it.\n\n2. The v2 API defines services for Cluster Discovery Service (CDS), Listener Discovery Service (LDS), Route Discovery Service (RDS), Endpoint Discovery Service (EDS), and Scoped Routes Discovery Service (SRDS).\n\n3. Multiple v2-versioned configuration extensions import from the core v2 API, including trace, metrics, rate limiting, and various network filters.\n\n4. The C++ code references v2 API types both in test harnesses (ensuring v2 symbols are available) and in implementation code for backward compatibility and API version handling.\n\n5. Some v3 proto files contain references to `envoy.api.v2` in their `previous_message_type` annotations to document migration from v2, but these were excluded from the count as they are v3 implementations documenting the migration path rather than active v2 API definitions.\n"}

# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

**Target Repositories (version-pinned mirrors):**

- `github.com/sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e$` filter

Scope ALL keyword_search/nls_search queries to these repos.
Use the repo name as the `repo` parameter for read_file/go_to_definition/find_references.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

**Sourcegraph Repositories:** `github.com/sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367`, `github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2`, `github.com/sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6`, `github.com/sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e`

  "text": "## Envoy v2 API Deprecated References\n\nThis analysis identified all files in the envoyproxy/envoy and envoyproxy/data-plane-api repositories that reference the deprecated Envoy v2 API namespace `envoy.api.v2`. The v2 xDS API was deprecated in Envoy 1.19 and replaced by the v3 API.\n\n### Summary of Findings\n\n#### Core v2 API Definition Files\nThe main body of deprecated v2 API definitions are located in the `api/envoy/api/v2/` directory hierarchy in the envoy repository (or `envoy/api/v2/` in the data-plane-api repository). These proto files contain the primary definitions of the v2 xDS API including:\n\n**Top-level xDS service definitions (39 files):**\n- `rds.proto`, `cds.proto`, `eds.proto`, `lds.proto`, `srds.proto`: Main xDS discovery service definitions\n- `route.proto`, `cluster.proto`, `listener.proto`, `endpoint.proto`, `scoped_route.proto`, `discovery.proto`: Core resource type definitions\n\n**Core package (`api/envoy/api/v2/core/`):**\nContains fundamental building blocks used by xDS resources, including:\n- `base.proto`, `address.proto`, `config_source.proto`: Basic configuration structures\n- `grpc_service.proto`, `health_check.proto`, `http_uri.proto`: Service-related configurations  \n- `backoff.proto`, `event_service_config.proto`, `socket_option.proto`: Advanced configuration options\n- `grpc_method_list.proto`, `protocol.proto`: Method and protocol definitions\n\n**Auth package (`api/envoy/api/v2/auth/`):**\n- `tls.proto`, `cert.proto`, `common.proto`, `secret.proto`: TLS and authentication configurations\n\n**Listener package (`api/envoy/api/v2/listener/`):**\n- `listener.proto`, `listener_components.proto`: Listener configuration components\n- `udp_listener_config.proto`, `quic_config.proto`: UDP and QUIC-specific configurations\n\n**Endpoint package (`api/envoy/api/v2/endpoint/`):**\n- `endpoint.proto`, `endpoint_components.proto`, `load_report.proto`: Endpoint and load-reporting configurations\n\n**Route package (`api/envoy/api/v2/route/`):**\n- `route.proto`, `route_components.proto`: Route configuration components\n\n**Cluster package (`api/envoy/api/v2/cluster/`):**\n- `circuit_breaker.proto`, `filter.proto`, `outlier_detection.proto`: Cluster-specific configurations\n\n**RateLimit package (`api/envoy/api/v2/ratelimit/`):**\n- `ratelimit.proto`: Rate limiting configuration\n\n#### Config Files Importing v2 API (26 files)\n\nThese files are v2-versioned config extensions that directly import from the v2 API namespace:\n\n**Trace and metrics services:**\n- `config/trace/v2/service.proto`, `config/trace/v2alpha/xray.proto`\n- `config/metrics/v2/metrics_service.proto`\n- `service/trace/v2/trace_service.proto`, `service/metrics/v2/metrics_service.proto`\n\n**Network filters:**\n- `config/filter/network/direct_response/v2/config.proto`\n- `config/filter/network/client_ssl_auth/v2/client_ssl_auth.proto`\n- `config/filter/network/local_rate_limit/v2alpha/local_rate_limit.proto`\n- `config/filter/network/ext_authz/v2/ext_authz.proto` (data-plane-api only)\n\n**Other configuration modules:**\n- `config/ratelimit/v2/rls.proto`: Rate limit service definition\n- `admin/v2alpha/listeners.proto`: Admin API for listeners\n- `config/retry/omit_host_metadata/v2/omit_host_metadata_config.proto`: Retry policy configuration\n- `config/grpc_credential/v2alpha/file_based_metadata.proto`: gRPC credential handling\n- `config/transport_socket/tap/v2alpha/tap.proto`: Tap transport socket (packet capture)\n\n**Data definitions:**\n- `data/tap/v2alpha/http.proto`: HTTP-specific tap data definitions\n- `service/tap/v2alpha/tap.proto`: Tap service definition\n\n#### C++ Source Files (7 files in envoy repository)\n\nThese C++ files contain references to the v2 API namespace in code:\n\n1. **Header files:**\n   - `test/config/v2_link_hacks.h`: Link hacks to ensure v2 API symbols are included\n   - `source/common/config/api_version.h`: API version handling utilities\n   - `envoy/config/grpc_mux.h`: gRPC API mux with v2 API documentation\n   - `envoy/upstream/host_description.h`: Host description with v2 load balancing weight references\n   - `envoy/upstream/upstream.h`: Upstream configuration with v2 endpoint references\n\n2. **Implementation files:**\n   - `api/test/build/build_test.cc`: Verification of v2 service and method names\n   - `test/test_common/utility_test.cc`: Tests using v2 type URLs\n   - `source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc`: Type URL documentation referencing v2\n   - `source/common/listener_manager/listener_manager_impl.cc`: Listener implementation with v2 API references\n\n#### Configuration Examples (1 file)\n- `configs/using_deprecated_config.yaml`: YAML configuration example using deprecated v2 API features\n\n### Repository Distribution\n\n**envoyproxy/envoy:** 61 files total\n- Proto definitions: 50 files\n- C++ source: 7 files  \n- Configuration examples: 1 file\n- Documentation: 3 files\n\n**envoyproxy/data-plane-api:** 48 files total\n- Proto definitions: 48 files (mirrors the proto structure from envoy but without C++ files)\n\n### Key Observations\n\n1. The v2 API is pervasive throughout the codebase, with the entire xDS discovery service and configuration system built around it.\n\n2. The v2 API defines services for Cluster Discovery Service (CDS), Listener Discovery Service (LDS), Route Discovery Service (RDS), Endpoint Discovery Service (EDS), and Scoped Routes Discovery Service (SRDS).\n\n3. Multiple v2-versioned configuration extensions import from the core v2 API, including trace, metrics, rate limiting, and various network filters.\n\n4. The C++ code references v2 API types both in test harnesses (ensuring v2 symbols are available) and in implementation code for backward compatibility and API version handling.\n\n5. Some v3 proto files contain references to `envoy.api.v2` in their `previous_message_type` annotations to document migration from v2, but these were excluded from the count as they are v3 implementations documenting the migration path rather than active v2 API definitions.\n"}
